bbc.title: Ayatollah Khamenei's iron grip on power in Iran comes to an end
bbc.description: Iranian state TV confirms the supreme leader has been killed on the first day of massive US and Israeli air strikes on the country.

bbc.title: What we know so far about the US-Israel attacks and Iran's retaliation
bbc.description: Iran responds by launching missiles and drones towards Israel and four Gulf Arab countries that host US military bases.

bbc.title: Iran says US and Israel strikes hit school killing 108
bbc.description: There is no confirmation by the US or Israel of the strike on the school located near elite military troops.

bbc.title: Luxury Dubai hotel hit as Iran launches retaliatory strikes across region
bbc.description: Smoke and flames rose from Dubai's Fairmont The Palm hotel as civilian targets came under fire along with the US military bases across the Middle East.

bbc.title: Where have Nepal's 'nepo kids' gone as corruption takes centre stage in election?
bbc.description: The excess of politicians' children was front and centre of the anger that drove protests that toppled the government last year.

bbc.title: Wellness peptide craze: Why people are injecting drugs 'not for human consumption'
bbc.description: Growing numbers of people are injecting unregulated peptides for health reasons - but one expert says they are "lab rats".

bbc.title: Why is WhatsApp's privacy policy facing a legal challenge in India?
bbc.description: A 2021 policy update requires users to share data with Meta for ads purposes to continue using the app.

bbc.title: At least 20 killed after military plane carrying banknotes crashes in Bolivia
bbc.description: Police fire tear gas to disperse crowds allegedly trying to take banknotes from the crash site.

wsj.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
wsj.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

wsj.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
wsj.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

wsj.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
wsj.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

wsj.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
wsj.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

wsj.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
wsj.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

wsj.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
wsj.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

wsj.title: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining Fighter-Jet Program, PM Meloni Says
wsj.description: The U.K., Italy and Japan have already partnered on the Global Combat Air Programme, which aims to put a new stealth fighter with supersonic capability in the skies by 2035.

wsj.title: Shippers Wary of Red Sea Routes Despite Houthi Pledge to End Targeting
wsj.description: The world’s top three container operators said they fear instability in Gaza and broader regional tensions mean continued danger.

wsj.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
wsj.description: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

wsj.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
wsj.description: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

wsj.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
wsj.description: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

wsj.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
wsj.description: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

wsj.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
wsj.description: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

wsj.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
wsj.description: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

wsj.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
wsj.description: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

wsj.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
wsj.description: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

bloomberg.title: OPEC+ Agrees in Principle to Larger Oil Hike, Delegates Say
bloomberg.description: OPEC+ agreed in principle to a slightly larger increase in oil production next month, delegates said, as a conflict sparked by US-Israeli strikes on Iran threatened to bolster a rally in crude prices.

bloomberg.title: Wall Street Turns to ‘Haven-First’ Strategy Amid Iran Crisis
bloomberg.description: The fast‑moving conflict across the Middle East is heightening investor anxiety and strengthening the case for safe‑haven trades such as Treasuries, gold and the Swiss franc.

bloomberg.title: US Job Growth Seen Moderating After Robust January
bloomberg.description: US payrolls growth probably settled back in February after the strongest month of hiring in a year, returning to a more moderate and likely sustainable pace of hiring.

bloomberg.title: Gulf Airlines Extend Flight Cancellations as Iran Targets Hubs
bloomberg.description: Airlines across the Persian Gulf extended their suspension of operations as retaliatory strikes from Iran enter a second day, causing major disruptions at some of the world’s busiest airports.

bloomberg.title: Japan Shipping Companies Suspend Operations in Persian Gulf

bloomberg.title: How Starmer Stumbled Into a French-Style Fix for Britain’s Woes
bloomberg.description: The UK’s growth plan is simple. It wants more investment to deliver higher productivity that lifts wages and boosts living standards. There is an unfortunate consequence, however: unemployment.

bloomberg.title: France’s CMA CGM Suspends Vessels’ Passage Through Suez Canal
bloomberg.description: France’s CMA CGM SA, the world’s third-largest container line, told vessels in the Persian Gulf to take shelter immediately and suspended passage through the Suez Canal.

bloomberg.title: Iran Supreme Leader Killed in US-Israel Attack as War Widens

bloomberg.title: Belgian Forces Seize Russian ‘Shadow Fleet’ Tanker in North Sea
